{"query":"Mattel autistic Barbie release date features ASAN","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1861,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2026/01/12/business/video/mattel-unveils-autistic-barbie-vrtc","title":"Mattel unveils autistic Barbie | CNN Business","content":"Mattel has unveiled its first autistic Barbie doll, featuring sensory-friendly accessories and design details aimed at better representing neurodivergent experiences.
The release follows other inclusive Barbies, including dolls representing blindness, Down’s syndrome and Type 1 diabetes.
The Centers for Disease Control has linked more than 1,600 cases of cyclospora across five states to Taco Bell.
They've spent $10K a month for daycare"},{"url":"https://abcnews.com/GMA/Living/advocacy-groups-react-after-mattel-introduces-1st-barbie/story?id=129132647","title":"Advocacy groups react after Mattel introduces 1st Barbie doll with autism - ABC News","content":"Advocacy groups react after Mattel introduces 1st Barbie doll with autism
Many celebrated the new doll from the global toy company, saying its introduction raises awareness of a condition that the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention estimates approximately 3.2% or about 1 in 31 children in the U.S. have.
"Autistic kids often feel excluded because they are different.
The advocacy group and nonprofit Autism Speaks also said the new doll "helps reinforce a positive self-image for autistic children."
The new Barbie doll, part of the Barbie Fashionistas collection, has features Mattel said reflects common experiences some with autism have or relate to, such as elbow and wrist articulation that can showcase stimming behaviors like hand flapping and a slanted gaze to indicate non-direct eye contact.
Mattel has sought to diversify its Barbie dolls in recent years and introduced a Barbie doll with Type 1 diabetes in July 2025 and a Barbie doll with Down syndrome in April 2023."},{"url":"https://shop.mattel.com/products/barbie-fashionistas-doll-in-purple-striped-dress-autistic-barbie-jjn58","title":"Barbie Doll | Mattel","content":"She comes with a working fidget spinner that she loves having handy when out and about, from hanging with friends to picnics in the park or going to the movies.
An included Augmentative and Alternative Communication tablet helps her express herself in ways other than talking, reflecting how this diverse line of Barbie Fashionistas dolls showcases bright and trendy styles that inspire endless storytelling possibilities for kids 3 years and up.
Doll Design: First Autistic Barbie doll created in partnership with the Autistic Self Advocacy Network (ASAN)
Barbie Fashionistas Doll #245 comes with noise-canceling headphones, a working fidget spinner, and an Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) tablet.
Barbie Fashionistas Doll #245 has flexible elbows and wrists so kids can play out stimming movements and expressive gestures.
The noise-canceling headphones help her relax when she’s sensitive to stimuli, and the fidget spinner really works for on-the-go sensory play.
